title: grewia macrophylla
intro: Grewia macrophylla, also known as the Elephant's Ear or Forest Mallow, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, native to tropical Asia and Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Grewia macrophylla include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy in various herbal preparations. The plant is often used in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
